Motion To Modify Order Regarding Distribution Of Ronald A Wilson'S Share Of This Estate To His Heirs; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Text Ruling

Motion To Modify Order Regarding Distribution Of Ronald A Wilson'S Share Of This Estate To His Heirs; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Continued To Jul-17-15 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for (Administrator) James Wolf. Laura Woodward for (Beneficiaries) Julie Wilson Rediger & Timothy Wilson.

The Court asked Ms. Woodward why a 13100 declaration was not submitted instead of filing two motions which incurred filing fees as well as attorney's fees. Ms. Woodward stated that she did not represent the beneficiaries when the order was issued, that she represented Kemp & Associates at that time, that she is not getting paid on this, that her clients in Illinois balked at having to file additional documents and that the assignments have been filed. Mr. Collins stated that he has checked the register of actions and does not see that the assignments were filed, that she had presented copies to him and that he thought she had filed them. Ms. Woodward stated that there is no code requiring heir finders to file assignments. The Court stated that it looked at the previous motion and did not see the Small Estate Affidavit filed, that it would not be too much to ask the administrator to provide a 13100 and that Mr. Collins had previously objected to checks being sent to Ms. Woodward. Mr. Collins stated that the declaration of Ms. Rediger had defects and that he had brought this to Ms. Woodward's attention. Ms. Woodward stated that there is a Small Estate Affidavit attached to the motion. The Court stated that distribution needs to be done pursuant to California law and that Ronald's assignment is to Kemp. Ms. Woodward provided a copy of the Small Estate Affidavit to the court for review and stated that she has tried to talk to her clients about signing a 13100 but one of them is very hard to explain things to. The Court requested a copy of Mr. Collins' declaration which was provided and reviewed and asked Ms. Woodward if a death certificate for the wife had been filed. Ms. Woodward responded that it is attached to her brief. The Court took a short recess and spoke with the probate examiner, stated that there are two issues, that there is not a 13100 declaration pursuant to California law but that we do have a death certificate, will and affidavit, that it is willing to consider instructing the administrator further on this, that our orders direct distribution to the heirs not to entities, that this motion can be continued to provide time for Ms. Woodward to file the assignments and take the matter under submission at that time. The court stated that the second issue is the concern of checks being made out to one person but being given to someone else. Ms. Woodward clarified that the checks would be going to her and not to Kemp, and submitted a proposed order. The Court took the Motion to Modify Order Regarding Distribution of Ronald A. Wilson's Share of this Estate under submission stating that it would have wanted a 13100 but that it does have the Small Estate Affidavit, will and death certificate.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Jung. (Not Reported)

Motion To Modify Order Regarding Distribution Of Ronald A Wilson'S Share Of This Estate To His Heirs; Points And Authorities
Off Calendar May-15-2015 Continued To Jun-19-15 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Motion To Compel Distribution Of R.A.Wilson'S Share Of This Estate To His Heirs, J. Wilson Rediger And T. D. Wilson; Points And Authorities.
Off Calendar Apr-08-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laura Woodward for (beneficiaries) Julie Rediger & Timothy Wilson. Charles Collins for (administrator) James Wolf.

Ms. Woodward stated that there was a previous order issued and that it was done without jurisdiction over the Illinois estate. The court stated that there has been no jurisdictional error and that what we are here for today is a motion which is not the proper avenue to get the result requested. Ms. Woodward stated that a modification of the prior order is necessary and asked if the parties could stipulate to distribution. The court stated that a stipulation would need to be properly submitted, that it cannot give legal advice and that it can only decide what is properly in front of it. The motion to compel distribution, etc. is off calendar without prejudice to whatever else is presented to the court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-11-2014 Continued To Jul-02-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for James Wolf.

The Court raised the issue of the copies of the checks from the first distribution indicating that they were negotiated by Kemp & Associates and stated that it does not see where the assignments have been filed. Mr. Collins stated that he has copies of the assignments, that he asked Ms. Woodward if she was going to comply with Probate Code 11604, that he received no response and that all of the checks were made out to the individuals. Mr. Collins also stated that he did not notice that Katherine's check was signed by Jack August, that Ronald Wilson had an assignment and is now deceased, and that the assignment is the reason for the deduction of $1,125.00 from Ronald's share. The issue of dealing with Ronald's share by way of 13100 provided his Estate meets the requirement was also discussed. The accounting petition was continued to July 2, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of the assignments, for filing of a noticed amendment indicating the corrected percentages to the six individuals as indicated in the Probate Examiner's notes and something as to who Ronald's share is going to.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Collins for James Wolf (Administrator).

Mr. Collins stated that the Controller's Office paid the Administrator cash on the claim for the property it was holding. Mr. Collins also stated that they were working on status of the stock in the company that went bankrupt, that Wells Fargo said that the stock sold for $12,000.00, that they thought it was resolved, and then Wells Fargo reversed it and said no that there was no sale, and proceeded to charge administrative fees of $1,000.00. In addition, Mr. Collins stated that he thinks he could do an Inventory and Appraisal by December. Status on close of the estate was continued to January 7, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
